articles.title: Introducing T7 template for the website
articles.description:
For Template 7, this document will primarily cover the following


  • How categories are organized on the Home page
  • How featured sections are catered
  • Option of article page with and without side navbar


Template Type and its Arrangements of Categories on the Home page 


We have 19 options to choose from, each tailored to accommodate different amounts and styles of content:


Type 1 | Minimum Number of Category's Post  - 7




Type 2 | Minimum Number ofCategory's Post - 10



Type 3 | Minimum Number of Category's Post - 5




Type 4 | Four columns | Minimum Number of Category's Post - 8



Type 4 | Three columns | Minimum Number of Category's Post - 6


 



Type 5 | Two columns | Minimum Number of Categorie's Post - 2




Type 5 | Three columns | Minimum Number of Category's Post - 3




Type 5 | Four columns | Minimum Number of Category's Post - 4



Type 5 | Six columns | Minimum Number of Category's Posts - 6




Type 6 | Minimum Number of Category's Posts - 12



Type 7 | Minimum Number of Category's  Posts - 12



Type 8 | Minimum Number of Category's Posts - 5




Type 9 | Minimum Number of Category's Posts - 12



Type 10 | Minimum Number of Category's  Posts - 12



Type 11 | Minimum Number of Category's Posts - 12



Type 12 (Without image ) | Minimum Number of Category's Posts - 10


Type 12 (With image ) | Minimum Number of Category's Posts - 10



Type 13 (Without image ) | Minimum Number of Category's Posts - 10




Type 13 (With image ) | Minimum Number of Category's Posts - 10





How featured sections are catered


We have 24 options to choose from, each tailored to accommodate different amounts and styles of content



Featured Type 1 | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 4,  Total - 4



Featured Type 2 | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 3,  Total - 6



Featured Type 3 | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 5,  Total - 4



Featured Type 4 | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 6,  Total -6



Featured Type 5 | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 10,  Total - 7 



Featured Type 6 | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 7




Featured Type 7 | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 10




Featured Type 8 | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 5






Featured Type 9 ( Four Columns) | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 8



Featured Type 9 (Three Columns) | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 6


Featured Type 10 ( Two Columns) | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 2



Featured Type 10 ( Three Columns) | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 3



Featured Type 10 ( Four Columns) | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 3



Featured Type 10 ( Six Columns) | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 6


Featured Type 11 |  Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 12



Featured Type 12 |  Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 12



Featured Type 13 | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 5


Featured Type 14 | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 12



Featured Type 15 | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 12



Featured Type 16 | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 12



Featured Type 17 (without image) | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 10



Featured Type 17 (with image) | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 10



Featured Type 18 (without image) | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 10



Featured Type 18 (with image) | Minimum Number of Posts: Featured - 10




Article page Customization


Article page without side navbar




Article page with side navbar

all_folders.name: Ads & Banners
all_folders.description: This folder assist with the Ads and Banners related information

articles.title: Ad Placement on the Article Page
articles.description:
This document provides detailed information about ad placement within the Article Page of the website. The following guidelines outline the placement and naming conventions for ads to ensure consistency and optimal user experience.

Ad Placement

1. Top of the Page

  • The first ad is loaded at the top of the page.

  • For the Article Page, the ad placement is denoted as:

    • desktop_ap_top for desktop users.

    • mobile_ap_top for mobile users.


2. In-Content Ads

  • In-content ads are displayed within the article content to enhance visibility and engagement.

  • Placement Details:

    1. The first in-content ad appears after the first paragraph.


    2. Subsequent ads appear after every third paragraph.


Important Consideration:

  • Headings are also counted as paragraphs when calculating ad placement.


For any further assistance create a ticket using help widget available in the dashboard or reach out to support@thepublive.com

In-Content Ads    In-content ads are displayed within the article content to enhance visibility and engagement. Important Consideration:    Headings are also counted as paragraphs when calculating ad placement.     For any further assistance create a ticket using help widget available in the dashboard or reach out to support@thepublive.com

category.name: Content Creation
category.description: This category is created to cater help docs that helps with content creation workflow

articles.title: How to make any article as Featured Posts?
articles.description:
This document is designed to help publishers to feature their posts, an excellent way to highlight important content on your website. Featured posts ensures they gain more visibility and remain prominently displayed at the top of your site.


Steps to make the post as featured:


Step 1: Log In and Navigate to the Published Section

  • Log in to the PubLive Dashboard.


  • Navigate to Post -> Click Published section to view all your published articles.




Step 2:Select and Feature an Article

  • Choose the articles you want to feature.
  • Click on the Set as Featured Post option.


Step 3: Confirm Featured Status

  • Once marked as featured, the selected article will:

    • Appear with a blue asterisk in the Published section.
    • Automatically move to the Featured section of the dashboard.


Step 4: Manage Featured Post Visibility

  • Use the drag-and-drop functionality to rearrange the order of featured articles.
  • The priority numbering is automatically assigned when the user rearranges the items.
  • Click Save after making changes to ensure the updated order reflects on the website.


Note:

  •  A maximum of 20 featured posts can be displayed in the Featured Posts section on the website. Ensure to prioritize your articles accordingly.
  • Each featured post has a Delete and Preview option visible in the Featured section.
  • Deleting an article from the Featured section will not delete the article itself. It will only remove the post from the featured list, keeping it available in the Published section.


Best Practices:

  • Prioritize High-Quality Content: Feature articles that are well-researched, engaging, and valuable to your audience. Highlighting subpar content can diminish the effectiveness of the feature.
  • Regularly Update Featured Posts: Keep the featured section dynamic by rotating articles frequently. Showcase seasonal or trending content to maintain audience interest.
  • Align with Business Goals: Select articles that align with your website’s objectives, such as driving sales, increasing awareness, or promoting specific campaigns.
  • Use Engaging Visuals: Ensure featured articles have eye-catching thumbnails and titles to immediately capture visitor attention.
  • Track Performance: Use analytics tools to monitor the traffic and engagement metrics of featured articles. Optimize your selection based on these insights.

Use Cases :

  • Product Launch Announcements: Showcase new product launches or updates to drive awareness and sales.
  • Trending Topics: Feature articles covering trending news, popular topics, or viral stories to boost traffic.
  • Evergreen Content: Promote timeless, high-value articles that continuously attract readership over time.
  • Event Promotions: Draw attention to upcoming events, webinars, or workshops by featuring related posts.
  • Content Series: Spotlight parts of a multi-article series, encouraging readers to explore related articles.

Benefits:

  • Enhanced Visibility: Featuring posts ensures important content remains prominently displayed, capturing the audience’s attention immediately upon visiting the website.
  • Increased Engagement: By highlighting compelling content, featured posts encourage users to click and interact more with the website.
  • Improved SEO Performance: Featuring frequently updated, high-quality articles can improve your website's search engine rankings by signaling relevance and freshness.
  • Audience Targeting: Allows publishers to direct readers to content that meets specific objectives, such as promotions or announcements.
  • Better Content Organization: Featured posts help create a structured and visually appealing layout, making it easier for readers to navigate.


All featured posts will appear at the top of your website in the order specified in the Featured section of the dashboard. This allows you to prioritize and control which articles are most visible to your audience.

articles.title: What is Photo Gallery and How it works?
articles.description:
The Photo Gallery feature enables users to showcase multiple images in a visually appealing layout, enhancing content presentation and engagement. It allows publishers to create immersive visual experiences, improving content visibility in search results and attracting more audience interaction.


How to Use the Photo Gallery

Step 1: Log in to Your Dashboard

  • Log in to the PubLive Dashboard page and enter your credentials.


Step 2: Create a New Photo Gallery

  • Go to Content Creation -> Photo Gallery, and start a new project.



Step 3: Creating Your First Gallery

  • Fill in the necessary information:
    • Title
    • English Title
    • Summary
    • Meta Description
    • Featured Image (option to hide if needed)



Step 4: Upload Images in Gallery Slides

  • Select an image from the media library and upload it into the slides.


  • Click the Upload Image button to upload images from your system to the media library.



  • Add image details such as caption, source, name, and alt text for better SEO and accessibility.


Step 5: Organize Your Gallery

  • Edit images anytime by clicking the Edit Icon.
  • Rearrange images by dragging and dropping them into your desired order.
  • Click the Delete Icon to remove an image from the slider.
  • Click the Preview Icon to open the image in a modal for better viewing.



Step 6: Set the post properties:
 
Step 7: Advanced Properties Explained:

  • Access Level: Use this field to specify if the articles/posts is for logged-in users or public access.
  • Custom Date: Use this field for backdating purposes.
  • Select Schema: Choose a schema for the article.
  • Canonical URL: Provide the canonical URL for this content.
  • Meta Title: The main title displayed in search engine results.
  • OG Title: The title used when your content is shared on social media.
  • OG Description: A brief summary shown when your content is shared on social media.
  • Twitter Title: The title specifically for Twitter shares.
  • Twitter Description: A brief description for Twitter shares.
  • Meta Keywords: These keywords provide information about a web page's content to search engines.
  • Meta News Keywords: Specifically used by Google News publishers to define keywords relevant to their articles.
  • Code Injection Head: Include custom code to be injected into the head section of your web pages.
  • Custom Style Injection: Apply custom CSS styles to your posts



Step 8: Preview & Publish

  • The content will only be updated, not republished on clicking Update button.


  • You can review the version history to restore any previous changes if needed.


  • You have the flexibility to schedule your post for publishing at your convenience.



Note: 

  • The Advanced Properties section is accessible exclusively to Admin & Owners to ensure security and prevent unauthorized modifications. Other users cannot view or edit this section.
  • Hide the featured image to showcase the photo gallery directly on the article page by enabling "Hide Featured Image" as shown below





Assisted “Fix it” Feature:

  • Monitor Warnings and keep track of your SEO score,following the recommendations for fixes.
  • The recommended SEO score should be (90+) for SEO purpose

 

As shown below, the visibility of the Photo gallery on the website



How Does This Feature Help Your Business?


  • Enhanced Visual Appeal: A photo gallery allows publishers to showcase their content in a visually appealing manner, attracting and retaining the attention of website visitors.

  • Improved User Engagement: High-quality images and an intuitive user interface encourage visitors to explore content further, increasing engagement and time spent on the website.

  • Effective Storytelling: A well-curated photo gallery can effectively tell a story or convey a message, capturing the audience's interest and facilitating better communication of ideas.

  • Increased Brand Visibility: By presenting content in an aesthetically pleasing manner, publishers can enhance their brand image and reputation, fostering a positive perception among visitors.

  • Optimized SEO: Image-rich content can improve search engine optimization (SEO) by attracting organic traffic through image search results. Additionally, optimizing image metadata can further enhance visibility in search engine rankings.

  • Social Media Promotion: Compelling images from the photo gallery can be easily shared on social media platforms, increasing brand exposure and driving traffic back to the website.

Best Practices :

  • Use High-Quality Images – Clear, high-resolution images enhance the user experience.
  • Maintain a Consistent Theme – Ensure images align with the article’s purpose or story.
  • Optimize Image Titles and Descriptions – Use descriptive and SEO-friendly text for better discoverability.
  • Ensure Mobile-Friendliness – Preview on multiple devices to maintain a seamless experience.
  • Limit the Number of Images – Avoid overloading the gallery; keep it concise and relevant.

Use Cases :

  • E-commerce – Showcase product collections with high-quality images.

  • Event Coverage – Highlight key moments from conferences, weddings, or exhibitions.

  • Travel & Tourism – Share travel experiences with stunning visuals.

  • Food & Recipes – Present step-by-step recipe visuals for better user engagement.

  • Real Estate & Architecture – Display property listings and architectural designs.

  • Fashion & Lifestyle – Feature style guides, fashion collections, or home decor ideas.

articles.title: How to enable IFTTT to automate share/post articles on social platforms
articles.description:
We can automate the process of sharing/post articles on social media platforms using IFTTT through RSS Feed. Below are the following steps for the same


Step 1


Create/ Sign-in to the IFTTT account and click create









Step 2


Click Add for "If This" as shown below



Step 3


Type RSS -> Select RSS Feed

Step 4


Select "New feed item"   




Step 5


Give the RSS feed URL in the Feed URL as shown below. It can be a 

  • Generic RSS Feed URL  - https://demo.thepublive.com/rss
    • This will post whenever any article is published in the website
  • Category level RSS Feed URL - https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/categories/news. 
    • This will post whenever any article is published in that specific category  


Click Create Trigger


Step 6


Now, click Add to add the social platform in which the post needs to be shared



Step 7


Search for social platform and click the service available 


Here, we are adding Facebook service (Facebook Pages) 



Step 8


Select Create a link post  


Click Connect and sign in to the Facebook account





















Step 9


Click Add Ingredient in Link URL field and select EntryURL & any ingredients available -> Click Create Action -> Continue to next page 



For example, if you select Add ingredient, you can pick something like EntryTitle to show the title of the feed item in the post. Can add message if required but not mandatory. This will post the article url with the image thumbnail in the Facebook   


Step 10


Rename the service/applet if required and optionally enable notifications each time it runs, and then press Finish. 




Similar to Facebook, other social media platforms, can be enabled in the same way


Other Reference Resources


https://www.lifewire.com/set-up-rss-feed-on-facebook-3486528

articles.title: How to Add your website to Google News Publication Center
articles.description:
Following are the steps to add your website to the Google publisher center


Step 1

  • Go to Google Publisher Center and enter the website URL and click 'Add' if this is the first time adding a website to the publication center



  • Else click 'Other Publication' from the top right dropdown as shown in the screenshot below 

 


Step 2

  • Add your Website Name, URL, and Location in the pop-up window and click Add Publication


Step 3

  • You will now see your website visible on the top right being added 
  • As a next step, click Open under Google News 



Step 4

  • Click Edit and start filling in the details


Step 5


Under General, select the appropriate publication category, country, and google properties (default value - allow all properties) and click Next




Step 6


In Content settings, click New Section to add a category and corresponding RSS feed



Step 7


Provide the following,

  • Section Title - Category/tag name/generic/featured/any
  • RSS feed - corresponding category/tag/generic/featured RSS feed URL
    • Category: https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/categories/category_slug
    • Tag: https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/tags/tag_slug
    • Generic: https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/
    • Featured: https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/featured/
  •  View access
    • default value "Anyone"

Then, Click Add



Similarly, add other categories as per your requirement in the above way



After some time, all articles will be available under each category RSS feed. Click refresh to see the changes


Note: After adding RSS feed URL, it may take some time for articles to be listed

 



Step 8

Next, go to the Review and Publish section. Click Review as shown in the screenshot below



Enter your name, email id, title(founder/director..etc), and organization in the pop-up form and select the checkbox and click Submit





Step 9


After completing all the above steps, click Publish

 


Once processing is completed, your website will be visible in Google News indicated as Live



Note: Once your account is Live, kindly share the Google News listing page URL with us to enable the Google News follow us button in your website




Reference

  • https://kinsta.com/blog/submit-to-google-news/
  • https://support.google.com/news/publisher-center/answer/9606481?hl=en
articles.desc_un_html: Following are the steps to add your website to the Google publisher center     Step 1   Go to Google Publisher Center and enter the website URL and click 'Add' if this is the first time adding a website to the publication center            Else click 'Other Publication' from the top right dropdown as shown in the screenshot below          Step 2   Add your Website Name, URL, and Location in the pop-up window and click Add Publication        Step 3   You will now see your website visible on the top right being added   As a next step, click Open under Google News            Step 4   Click Edit and start filling in the details         Step 5     Under General, select the appropriate publication category, country, and google properties (default value - allow all properties) and click Next             Step 6     In Content settings, click New Section to add a category and corresponding RSS feed          Step 7     Provide the following,   Section Title - Category/tag name/generic/featured/any  RSS feed - corresponding category/tag/generic/featured RSS feed URL  Category: https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/categories/category_slug  Tag: https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/tags/tag_slug  Generic: https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/  Featured: https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/featured/     View access  default value "Anyone"     Then, Click Add          Similarly, add other categories as per your requirement in the above way          After some time, all articles will be available under each category RSS feed. Click refresh to see the changes     Note: After adding RSS feed URL, it may take some time for articles to be listed            Step 8  Next, go to the Review and Publish section. Click Review as shown in the screenshot below          Enter your name, email id, title(founder/director..etc), and organization in the pop-up form and select the checkbox and click Submit                Step 9     After completing all the above steps, click Publish          Once processing is completed, your website will be visible in Google News indicated as Live          Note: Once your account is Live, kindly share the Google News listing page URL with us to enable the Google News follow us button in your website           Reference   https://kinsta.com/blog/submit-to-google-news/  https://support.google.com/news/publisher-center/answer/9606481?hl=en

articles.title: How to use Typeform with PubLive
articles.description:
The integration between PubLive and Typeform allows publishers to create engaging forms and surveys that can be seamlessly embedded into their PubLive pages. This integration enhances user interaction and data collection, making it easier to gather feedback, conduct surveys, and engage with your audience. 


About Typeform

Typeform is a versatile online tool that enables users to create interactive and visually appealing forms, surveys, quizzes, and more. Known for its user-friendly design and engaging interface, Typeform transforms data collection into a seamless and enjoyable experience for both creators and respondents.


Key Features

  1. Seamless Embedding:

    • Easily embed Typeform forms and surveys into your PubLive pages without any coding knowledge.
    • Customize the appearance to match your site’s design and branding.

  2. Interactive and Engaging Forms:

    • Use Typeform's interactive and visually appealing forms to enhance user engagement.
    • Incorporate various question types, including multiple choice, short answer, and rating scales.

  3. Real-Time Data Collection:

    • Collect responses in real time and view them directly within your Typeform dashboard.

  4. Enhanced User Experience:

    • Provide a smooth and engaging experience for your audience, encouraging higher response rates.
    • Utilize Typeform’s mobile-friendly design for seamless user interaction on any device.


Setup Instructions

Create a Typeform Account:

  • If you don’t already have a Typeform account, sign up at Typeform.

Create Your Form or Survey:

  • Design your form or survey using Typeform’s intuitive builder.
  • Customize the questions, layout, and design to match your requirements.

Get the Embed Code:

  • Once your form is ready, navigate to the “Share” tab of the form builder in Typeform.

  • Press the Get the code button and copy the embed code provided. 


Embed in PubLive:

  • Log in to your PubLive account.
  • Navigate to the page where you want to embed the form.
  • Use the HTML embed option by going to Insert -> Media -> Embed to paste the Typeform embed code into the desired location on your page. 


Publish and Test:

  • Save your changes and publish the page.
  • Test the embedded form to ensure it displays correctly and functions as intended.


Automation to submissions


To make your form more powerful, consider adding some automation by using Zapier to process your form submissions. There are tons of things you can do with it, but here is the link to help you get started


How to use PubLive and Zapier together? 

Zapier Integration


Benefits

  1. Improved User Engagement:

    • Engage your audience with interactive forms and surveys that are easy to fill out and visually appealing.
    • Increase participation and response rates with Typeform's user-friendly interface.

  2. Efficient Data Collection:

    • Collect valuable data and feedback from your audience quickly and efficiently.
    • Use the insights gathered to make informed decisions and improve your content strategy.

  3. Customization and Flexibility:

    • Customize forms to fit your specific needs and branding requirements.
    • Use various question types to gather the information you need effectively.

  4. Enhanced Workflow Integration:

    • Integrate Typeform responses with other tools and workflows for seamless data processing and analysis.
    • Automate follow-ups and responses based on the data collected.


The integration between PubLive and Typeform offers a powerful tool for publishers to engage with their audience, collect valuable data, and enhance the overall user experience. By embedding interactive and visually appealing forms, publishers can gather insights and feedback efficiently, helping to improve content strategy and audience interaction.

articles.title: How to embed YouTube videos within PubLive Editor
articles.description:
Youtube videos can be embedded in the posts in Editor using two ways. Following are the methods, 


  • Smart embed - Copy paste URL of the YT video
  • Embed code - Copy paste the embed code in Insert/Media


Get Started


Through Smart embed


Click share in Youtube video and select the URL as shown below




Paste in Editor, which will give the preview of the same




Through Embed Code


Step 1: Click share in Youtube video and select embed code option



Step 2: Copy the embed code  



Step 3: In the dashboard, navigate to the create/edit page where you want to embed the form.


Use the HTML embed option by going to Insert -> Media -> Embed to paste the YouTube embed code into the desired location on your page and click Save



On Save, shows the video embedded in the posts within the Editor as shown below




Step 4: Save your changes and publish the post
articles.desc_un_html: Youtube videos can be embedded in the posts in Editor using two ways. Following are the methods,       Smart embed - Copy paste URL of the YT video  Embed code - Copy paste the embed code in Insert/Media      Get Started     Through Smart embed     Click share in Youtube video and select the URL as shown below             Paste in Editor, which will give the preview of the same             Through Embed Code     Step 1: Click share in Youtube video and select embed code option          Step 2: Copy the embed code            Step 3: In the dashboard, navigate to the create/edit page where you want to embed the form.     Use the HTML embed option by going to Insert -> Media -> Embed to paste the YouTube embed code into the desired location on your page and click Save        On Save, shows the video embedded in the posts within the Editor as shown below           Step 4: Save your changes and publish the post

articles.title: Giving Access In Google Analytics
articles.description:
To give access to your Google Analytics account, you can follow these steps:



 

Step 1

  • Sign in to your Google Analytics account
  • Click on the Admin tab in the navigation menu


Step 2

  • Under the "Account" section, select the account that you want to give access to
  • Click “Account User Management.”


Step 3

  • Click the blue  +  to add a new user to your account
  • Enter the email address
  • Select "Notify new users by email" for the person to receive the invite
  • Set roles as shown below for the member


Here are the options

  • Administrator: Grants complete control over the Google Analytics dashboard, from the account, property, and view levels
  • Editor: Allows the user to edit anything within the account, except for user settings
  • Analyst: Works like a Viewer role (below), but with the ability to make and edit shared items like dashboards
  • Viewer: Can view all settings and data on an account, but can’t edit or share anything
  • None: This is a role, but without any abilities, perhaps used for someone you intend to add in the future





Reference

  • https://themeisle.com/blog/how-to-add-user-to-google-analytics/

articles.title: How Pages section of the dashboard works?
articles.description:
Pages section can be used to create/edit/delete static pages like contact us, about us, etc. This section is available on the left side menu in the PubLive Dashboard as shown below




To create a new static page


Step 1

  • Click + button to create a new static page



Step 2


Enter the Page name, meta title (page name), meta description (brief description), and Content (content for the page), and click Save




Changes will be saved and the respective page will be created and available on the website.


To delete the existing page


Click Delete to delete any existing page
articles.desc_un_html: Pages section can be used to create/edit/delete static pages like contact us, about us, etc. This section is available on the left side menu in the PubLive Dashboard as shown below             To create a new static page     Step 1   Click + button to create a new static page           Step 2     Enter the Page name, meta title (page name), meta description (brief description), and Content (content for the page), and click Save             Changes will be saved and the respective page will be created and available on the website.     To delete the existing page     Click Delete to delete any existing page
